channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Uganda records one imported Ebola death after new outbreak in eastern DR Congo
item.description: Health authorities in the region have confirmed a cross-border Ebola incident after the Africa Centres for Disease Control and Prevention verified a new outbreak in the Ituri province of the Democrati...

item.title: Quezon City celebrates the return of La Loma Lechon Festival
item.description: Quezon City is set to bring back the beloved La Loma Lechon Festival, a popular event that highlights the city's rich culinary tradition, particularly its famous roasted pig, or lechon. The festival, ...

item.title: Patrick Cripps reflects on challenging AFL season and expresses loyalty to Carlton
item.description: Carlton captain Patrick Cripps has spoken openly about the tumultuous start to the 2026 AFL season, describing it as ‘career-defining’. The Blues endured a difficult 1-8 start, highlighted by repeated...

item.title: Rohit Pawar Criticises CJI’s Statement on Youth as Intolerant and Hurtful
item.description: Mumbai-based NCP leader Rohit Pawar expressed strong disapproval over Chief Justice of India Surya Kant's recent comment comparing some unemployed youth to cockroaches and parasites. The remark, made ...

item.title: Former banker turns coconut farming into jobs and value-added products
item.description: A 37-year-old entrepreneur has moved from the banking sector into coconut processing and farming, building a business that is creating income and jobs in the coastal economy. Her journey shows how agr...

item.title: US Schools Face Budget Strain as Diesel Prices Surge Due to Iran Conflict
item.description: Since the Iran war began in late February 2026, diesel prices have surged dramatically, putting immense pressure on US school budgets. School districts from Washington to Texas are struggling to maint...

item.title: Gbenga Daniel Insists on Free and Fair APC Primaries in Ogun East
item.description: Senator Gbenga Daniel of Ogun East don yan say e go only participate for primary elections wey dey free, fair and credible. E talk say e no go allow any election wey outcome don already dey arranged. ...

item.title: Deadline for NGOs to Join Public Benefit Organisations Extended by One Year
item.description: The Cabinet Secretary for Interior, Kipchumba Murkomen, has officially extended the deadline for all Non-Governmental Organisations (NGOs) to register under the Public Benefit Organisations (PBO) fram...

item.title: Samsung Workers Clash Over AI Profits and Bonus Distribution
item.description: In South Korea, Samsung Electronics is facing a potential 18-day strike as over 45,000 workers protest bonus payouts amid the AI industry boom. The dispute highlights a major internal conflict over ho...

item.title: Exploring 40 Iconic Film Posters from the 1930s
item.description: The 1930s marked a significant period in cinematic history, often referred to as the 'Golden Age' of film posters. During this era, studios transitioned from using simple promotional materials to crea...

item.title: Review: College Backbenchers Fight Zombies in Quirky Hindi Comedy
item.description: Indian Institute of Zombies is a Hindi comedy-thriller directed by Alok Kumar Dwivedi and Gaganjeet Singh, now playing in cinemas. The film is set in an IIT-type engineering college where an evil scie...

item.title: Tanzania calls on banks to expand financial access for unbanked population
item.description: Tanzania is facing a significant challenge in financial inclusion as about 78 percent of its population remain unbanked. Financial experts and policymakers are urging commercial banks to expand their ...

item.title: Supporters flock to Glasgow ahead of crucial Scottish Premiership title match
item.description: Thousands of football fans have descended on Glasgow for one of the most anticipated Scottish Premiership title deciders in recent years. Hearts can clinch their first league title in 66 years with a ...

item.title: CDC provides support as Ebola outbreak confirmed in DRC and Uganda
item.description: US health authorities are closely monitoring Ebola outbreaks in both the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) and Uganda, offering technical assistance to local governments. The DRC has reported 246...

item.title: Samsung Exynos 2700 to Use Side-by-Side Packaging Instead of WLP for Galaxy S27
item.description: Samsung's upcoming Exynos 2700 chipset, expected to power the Galaxy S27 and S27+, may feature a significant change in its packaging design. The company is reportedly moving away from Fan-Out Wafer-Le...

item.title: Court to Decide on NCC Leader Fadiel Adams’ Bail Amid Obstruction of Justice Allegations
item.description: The future of National Coloured Congress leader and MP Fadiel Adams is currently in the hands of the Pinetown Magistrates' Court in KwaZulu-Natal, which is set to rule on his bail application on Frida...

item.title: Major Events in the Philippines and Around the World: May 9–15, 2026
item.description: From May 9 to 15, 2026, significant events unfolded both in the Philippines and internationally. In Manila, the House of Representatives approved the justice committee report confirming probable cause...

item.title: Understanding the Intense Dislike Towards Arsenal and Mikel Arteta
item.description: Arsenal’s success in English football has provoked strong reactions from fans and neutrals alike, with many expressing genuine dislike for the club and its manager, Mikel Arteta. Despite being one of ...

item.title: Michael Olunga Opens State-of-the-Art Football Academy in Homa Bay
item.description: Kenyan football star and Harambee Stars captain Michael Olunga has realised his dream of establishing a modern football academy in Homa Bay County. The Michael Olunga Football Academy (MOFA), located ...

item.title: Elizabeth I’s Final Days: A Reflection of Control and Solitude
item.description: In March 1603, Queen Elizabeth I of England spent her final days seated on cushions in her Richmond Palace chamber, refusing sleep and sustenance. Historical accounts from Venetian envoy Scaramelli an...
